message:
feat(cli): improve agents command output and refine storage blocked cache handling

- Enhance agents subcommand with additional output fields and formatting
- Simplify sqlite storage layer logic
- Add storage_blocked_cache test coverage
- Update beads issue metadata

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"beads_rust-554","title":"Feature: SQLite Storage Layer Core","description":"# SQLite Storage Layer Core

## Purpose
Implement the storage interface with classic bd semantics: transaction discipline, dirty tracking, blocked cache, and efficient list/search queries.

## Transaction Discipline (critical)
Every mutation runs in **BEGIN IMMEDIATE** transaction and must:
1. Apply change (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE)
2. Insert audit event
3. Mark dirty (issue_id and often depends_on_id)
4. Invalidate/rebuild blocked cache if status/dependency changed

## Required Operations
- Issue CRUD + reopen + delete/tombstone
- Dependency add/remove/list/tree/cycles
- Label add/remove/list/list-all
- Comments add/list
- Events add/list (DESC ordering)
- List/Search with filters (LIKE-based)
- Ready/Blocked queries (blocked cache)
- Dirty tracking + export hash ops
- Config/metadata get/set
- Child counters for hierarchical IDs

## Performance Patterns
- Batch label/comment/dep lookups for list/search outputs.
- Cache blocked set in `blocked_issues_cache`.
- Use indexes from schema bead.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Mutation steps atomic with proper event + dirty + cache invalidation.
- `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` used for writes; retry on SQLITE_BUSY.
- Query semantics match bd (ordering and filters).

## Tests
- Transaction atomicity: events + dirty marker on mutation.
- Ready/blocked cache invalidation on dependency/status changes.
- Batch query correctness.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T06:14:08.177871945Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T08:44:07.182599044Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T08:44:07.182599044Z","close_reason":"Implemented SqliteStorage, open, transaction protocol, and create_issue in src/storage/sqlite.rs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}

 {"id":"beads_rust-56d","title":"Git Conflict Detection System","description":"## Overview
Implement detection of git merge conflicts in JSONL files. Before importing, br must check for conflict markers and refuse to import corrupted files.

## Git Conflict Markers
When git fails to auto-merge, it leaves markers:
```
<<<<<<< HEAD
{"id":"bd-abc12","title":"Local version",...}
=======
{"id":"bd-abc12","title":"Remote version",...}
>>>>>>> feature-branch
```

## Technical Requirements

### Conflict Detection
```rust
pub fn detect_git_conflicts(path: &Path) -> Result<Vec<ConflictInfo>> {
    let content = fs::read_to_string(path)?;
    let mut conflicts = Vec::new();
    let mut in_conflict = false;
    let mut conflict_start = 0;
    
    for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {
        if line.starts_with("<<<<<<<") {
            in_conflict = true;
            conflict_start = line_num + 1;
        } else if line.starts_with(">>>>>>>") {
            if in_conflict {
                conflicts.push(ConflictInfo {
                    file: path.to_path_buf(),
                    start_line: conflict_start,
                    end_line: line_num + 1,
                    marker: line.to_string(),
                });
                in_conflict = false;
            }
        }
    }
    
    // Unclosed conflict (corrupted file)
    if in_conflict {
        conflicts.push(ConflictInfo {
            file: path.to_path_buf(),
            start_line: conflict_start,
            end_line: content.lines().count(),
            marker: "unclosed conflict".into(),
        });
    }
    
    Ok(conflicts)
}

pub struct ConflictInfo {
    pub file: PathBuf,
    pub start_line: usize,
    pub end_line: usize,
    pub marker: String,
}
```

### Pre-Import Check
```rust
pub fn check_jsonl_for_conflicts(beads_dir: &Path) -> Result<Vec<ConflictInfo>> {
    let mut all_conflicts = Vec::new();
    
    // Check all JSONL files
    for file in ["issues.jsonl", "dependencies.jsonl", "labels.jsonl", "comments.jsonl"] {
        let path = beads_dir.join(file);
        if path.exists() {
            let conflicts = detect_git_conflicts(&path)?;
            all_conflicts.extend(conflicts);
        }
    }
    
    // Also check metadata.json
    let metadata_path = beads_dir.join("metadata.json");
    if metadata_path.exists() {
        let conflicts = detect_git_conflicts(&metadata_path)?;
        all_conflicts.extend(conflicts);
    }
    
    Ok(all_conflicts)
}
```

### Import Guard
```rust
pub fn import_jsonl(&mut self, beads_dir: &Path) -> Result<ImportStats> {
    // Check for conflicts FIRST
    let conflicts = check_jsonl_for_conflicts(beads_dir)?;
    if !conflicts.is_empty() {
        return Err(BeadsError::GitConflicts {
            conflicts,
            hint: "Run \"git mergetool\" or manually resolve conflicts, then retry".into(),
        });
    }
    
    // Safe to import
    self.do_import(beads_dir)
}
```

### Error Message
```rust
impl std::fmt::Display for BeadsError {
    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter) -> std::fmt::Result {
        match self {
            BeadsError::GitConflicts { conflicts, hint } => {
                writeln!(f, "Cannot import: {} git conflict(s) detected:", conflicts.len())?;
                for c in conflicts {
                    writeln!(f, "  {}:{}-{} {}", 
                        c.file.display(), c.start_line, c.end_line, c.marker)?;
                }
                writeln!(f, "\n{}", hint)?;
                Ok(())
            }
            // ... other variants
        }
    }
}
```

### CLI Output
```
$ br sync
Error: Cannot import: 2 git conflict(s) detected:
  .beads/issues.jsonl:15-23 >>>>>>> feature-branch
  .beads/issues.jsonl:45-52 >>>>>>> feature-branch

Resolve conflicts with "git mergetool" or edit files manually, then retry.
```

### Marker Patterns
```rust
const CONFLICT_START: &str = "<<<<<<<";
const CONFLICT_SEPARATOR: &str = "=======";
const CONFLICT_END: &str = ">>>>>>>";

fn is_conflict_marker(line: &str) -> Option<ConflictMarkerType> {
    if line.starts_with(CONFLICT_START) {
        Some(ConflictMarkerType::Start)
    } else if line.starts_with(CONFLICT_SEPARATOR) {
        Some(ConflictMarkerType::Separator)
    } else if line.starts_with(CONFLICT_END) {
        Some(ConflictMarkerType::End)
    } else {
        None
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detect <<<<<<< markers
- [ ] Detect ======= markers
- [ ] Detect >>>>>>> markers
- [ ] Report line numbers of conflicts
- [ ] Report which branch marker is from
- [ ] Check all JSONL files
- [ ] Check metadata.json
- [ ] Block import if conflicts detected
- [ ] Clear error message with resolution hint
- [ ] Handle unclosed conflicts (corrupted)

## Unit Tests
- Clean file returns no conflicts
- Single conflict detected
- Multiple conflicts in same file
- Conflicts in different files
- Unclosed conflict detected
- Import blocked by conflicts
- Line numbers accurate
- Branch names extracted from markers

## Dependencies
- JSONL Import Implementation

## Rationale
Git merge conflicts corrupt JSONL files, making them invalid JSON. Detecting conflicts before import prevents cryptic JSON parsing errors and data loss. Clear error messages guide users to resolve conflicts properly.","notes":"ASSESSMENT (2026-01-17): Feature is COMPLETE and fully implemented.

✅ IMPLEMENTED:
- All 3 conflict markers detected (<<<<<<<, =======, >>>>>>>)
- Line numbers reported (ConflictMarker.line)
- Branch names extracted (ConflictMarker.branch)
- Import blocked on conflict markers (ensure_no_conflict_markers)
- Clear error message with resolution hint
- Unit tests cover all marker types, line numbers, branch extraction

⚪ N/A (architecture difference):
- Multiple JSONL files: beads_rust uses single issues.jsonl
- metadata.json checking: it's config, not data

Core safety feature WORKS - any conflict marker blocks import.

 {"id":"beads_rust-59y","title":"Feature: Database Schema & Migrations","description":"# Database Schema & Migrations (SQLite)

## Purpose
Implement a schema **compatible with classic bd**. The Rust DB must match tables/columns/indexes/constraints so bd and br can operate on the same `.beads` directory.

## Core Tables (classic)
### issues
Fields (subset):
- Core: `id`, `content_hash`, `title`, `description`, `design`, `acceptance_criteria`, `notes`
- Workflow: `status`, `priority`, `issue_type`, `assignee`, `owner`, `estimated_minutes`
- Timestamps: `created_at`, `created_by`, `updated_at`, `closed_at`, `close_reason`, `closed_by_session`
- Scheduling: `due_at`, `defer_until`
- Tombstone: `deleted_at`, `deleted_by`, `delete_reason`, `original_type`
- Compaction: `compaction_level`, `compacted_at`, `compacted_at_commit`, `original_size`
- Messaging: `sender`, `ephemeral`
- Context: `pinned`, `is_template`
- Federation: `source_system`, `external_ref`

Constraints:
- Title length 1..500
- Priority 0..4
- Closed-at invariant (closed -> closed_at not null; non-closed -> closed_at null)

### dependencies
- PK `(issue_id, depends_on_id)`
- `depends_on_id` has **no FK** (allows `external:*` refs)
- `type`, `created_at`, `created_by`, `metadata`, `thread_id`

### labels, comments, events, config, metadata
- `labels` unique `(issue_id, label)`
- `comments` ordered by `created_at`
- `events` used for audit; not exported

### dirty_issues, export_hashes, blocked_issues_cache, child_counters
- `dirty_issues` tracks changes for incremental export
- `export_hashes` stores content hashes at export
- `blocked_issues_cache` materializes blocked IDs (+ blocked_by JSON per spec)
- `child_counters` tracks next child number per parent

## Indexes (must match bd)
- `issues.status`, `issues.priority`, `issues.issue_type`, `issues.assignee`, `issues.created_at`, `issues.updated_at`
- `dependencies.issue_id`, `dependencies.depends_on_id`, `dependencies.type`
- `labels.label`, `labels.issue_id`
- `events.issue_id`, `events.created_at`
- `dirty_issues.marked_at`
- `issues.external_ref` unique (non-null)

## Migration Strategy
- Legacy bd uses **idempotent migrations**, no `schema_migrations` table.
- For br: either
  - build a **consolidated schema** that already includes all classic fields, then
  - apply **minimal** idempotent migrations for forward compatibility.
- Do **not** include Gastown/HOP fields in schema.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `PRAGMA table_info`/index lists match bd for classic fields.
- Constraints enforce title length, priority range, closed-at invariant.

## Tests
- Schema snapshot tests comparing to bd (`PRAGMA table_info` + indexes).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T06:14:08.437783618Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T08:40:25.708253580Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T08:40:25.708253580Z","close_reason":"Implemented schema. Forced close because dependency direction in tracker seems inverted (Schema is prereq for Storage).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-5bg","title":"stale Command (age-based filtering)","description":"# stale Command (age-based filtering)

## Purpose
Report issues not updated in N days with optional status filters and clear JSON/text output.

## CLI
```
br stale [--days N] [--status ...]
```
Defaults:
- `--days` default = 30
- default status filter = open/in_progress unless overridden

## Behavior
- Uses `updated_at` to compute staleness (days since update).
- Supports status filters (comma-separated or repeated).
- Ordering: oldest updates first (most stale at top).

## Output
- JSON: array of Issue objects.
- Text: header `Stale issues (N not updated in D+ days):` with numbered list,
  including status, days stale, and assignee when present.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Correct default of 30 days and status filters.
- Accurate days-stale calculation from `updated_at`.
- JSON shape matches bd.

## Tests
- Issues with varying updated_at; verify threshold and ordering.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:04:24.103857464Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T16:24:04.829322915Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T16:24:04.829322915Z","close_reason":"stale command fully implemented with unit tests and CLI verified working","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-5fa","title":"Implement user configuration system (.brrc / config.toml)","description":"# User Configuration System

## Purpose
Allow users to customize br's behavior through configuration files, environment variables, and command-line flags with a clear precedence order.

## Why This Matters
- Users have different preferences (colors, defaults, paths)
- Teams may want consistent configuration
- AI agents may need specific output formatting
- Power users expect configurability

## Configuration Precedence (highest to lowest)
1. Command-line flags (always win)
2. Environment variables (`BR_*`)
3. Project config (`.beads/config.toml`)
4. User config (`~/.config/br/config.toml` or `~/.brrc`)
5. System defaults

## Configurable Options

### Display Options
```toml
[display]
color = "auto"  # auto | always | never
format = "human"  # human | json | compact
pager = true  # Use pager for long output
unicode = true  # Use unicode symbols (✓, ○, etc.)
relative_dates = true  # "2 hours ago" vs "2026-01-16T10:30:00Z"
```

### Default Values
```toml
[defaults]
priority = 2  # Default priority for new issues (0-4)
type = "task"  # Default type for new issues
status = "open"  # Default status for new issues
```

### Paths
```toml
[paths]
database = ".beads/beads.db"  # Relative to project root
jsonl = ".beads/issues.jsonl"  # Sync file location
```

### Behavior
```toml
[behavior]
auto_sync = false  # Auto-sync after mutations
confirm_destructive = true  # Confirm before delete/close
check_updates = "weekly"  # never | daily | weekly | always
```

### Agent Mode
```toml
[agent]
structured_errors = true  # Always use structured JSON errors
verbose_hints = true  # Include detailed hints in errors
```

## Environment Variables
```bash
BR_COLOR=never
BR_FORMAT=json
BR_DATABASE=/custom/path/beads.db
BR_DEFAULT_PRIORITY=1
BR_NO_PAGER=1
```

## Implementation

### Config Loading
```rust
use figment::{Figment, providers::{Toml, Env, Serialized}};

#[derive(Debug, Deserialize, Serialize)]
pub struct Config {
    pub display: DisplayConfig,
    pub defaults: DefaultsConfig,
    pub paths: PathsConfig,
    pub behavior: BehaviorConfig,
    pub agent: AgentConfig,
}

impl Config {
    pub fn load() -> Result<Self> {
        Figment::new()
            .merge(Serialized::defaults(Config::default()))
            .merge(Toml::file("~/.config/br/config.toml").nested())
            .merge(Toml::file(".beads/config.toml").nested())
            .merge(Env::prefixed("BR_").split("_"))
            .extract()
    }
}
```

### Config Command
```bash
br config              # Show current config (merged)
br config --list       # List all options
br config --get key    # Get specific value
br config --set key=value  # Set in user config
br config --edit       # Open config in $EDITOR
br config --path       # Show config file path
```

## Files to Create
- `src/config/mod.rs` - Config loading and types
- `src/config/defaults.rs` - Default values
- `src/cli/commands/config.rs` - Config command

## Cargo.toml Additions
```toml
[dependencies]
figment = { version = "0.10", features = ["toml", "env"] }
directories = "5.0"  # For XDG paths
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Config loads from all sources with correct precedence
- [ ] `br config` displays merged configuration
- [ ] `br config --set` modifies user config file
- [ ] Environment variables override config file
- [ ] Command-line flags override everything
- [ ] Missing config file is not an error
- [ ] Invalid config produces helpful error message

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_config_precedence() {
    // Set env var
    std::env::set_var("BR_DEFAULT_PRIORITY", "1");
    
    // Write project config with priority=3
    write_config(".beads/config.toml", "[defaults]\npriority = 3");
    
    let config = Config::load().unwrap();
    // Env should win
    assert_eq\!(config.defaults.priority, 1);
}

#[test]
fn test_config_defaults() {
    let config = Config::default();
    assert_eq\!(config.defaults.priority, 2);
    assert_eq\!(config.display.color, ColorChoice::Auto);
}
```

### E2E Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_config_affects_output() {
    // Set no-color via env
    let output = Command::new("br")
        .env("BR_COLOR", "never")
        .args(["list"])
        .output()
        .unwrap();
    
    // Should have no ANSI escape codes
    assert\!(\!String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).contains("\x1b["));
}
```

## Logging
- Log which config files were loaded
- Log config merge order
- Log any config parsing warnings","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverValley","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T20:21:52.027170853Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T22:38:50.100313876Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T22:38:50.100313876Z","close_reason":"Implemented br config command with full subcommand support: show merged config, --list options, --get/--set values, --edit to open editor, --path to show config paths, --project/--user for layer-specific views. +{"id":"beads_rust-ok70","title":"Fix blocked cache rebuild SQL parse error after issue writes","description":"Observed in the repo-local binary after verifying and closing beads_rust-586v. `./target/debug/br update beads_rust-586v --status in_progress --json` fails with `Database error: SQL error at offset 13: unexpected token at start of statement`. `./target/debug/br close beads_rust-586v ... --json` successfully persists the close, then fails while rebuilding the blocked cache with the same SQL parse error. Ready/show/doctor continue to work. This likely lives in the write-side cache rebuild / post-mutation path rather than markdown import itself.

Repro:
1. `./target/debug/br update beads_rust-586v --status in_progress --json`
2. `./target/debug/br close beads_rust-586v --reason ... --json`

Acceptance:
- update/close succeed without SQL parse errors
- blocked cache rebuild completes successfully after writes
- regression coverage exercises the current failure path","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:13:34.527928302Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T00:13:34.527928302Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"beads_rust-oqa","title":"External dependency resolution (external:<project>:<capability>)","description":"# External Dependency Resolution (external:<project>:<capability>)

## Purpose
Support classic external dependency semantics for ready/blocked and dependency trees.

## Encoding
- External dependency ID: `external:<project>:<capability>`
- Satisfied when external project has a **closed** issue labeled `provides:<capability>`.

## Config
- `external_projects` in config.yaml maps project name → path.

## Behavior
- External deps are **not** stored in blocked cache.
- Evaluated at query time (ready/blocked/tree) to avoid multi-DB work during cache rebuild.
- Open each external project DB **once per project**, batch by capability.

## Output
- In dep tree, synthesize external leaf nodes:
  - status `closed` if satisfied, else `blocked`
  - title prefixed `✓` or `⏳`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Ready/blocked filters respect external deps.
- External deps appear in dep tree (down direction only).

## Tests
- Mock external project with provides labels.
- Unresolved external deps remain blocking.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:03:46.203704905Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T03:53:40.739867167Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T03:53:40.739867167Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-oqw","title":"Shell Completions","description":"## Overview
Implement shell completion scripts for bash, zsh, fish, and PowerShell using clap_complete. This enables tab-completion for commands, options, and issue IDs.

## Technical Requirements

### Clap Complete Integration
```rust
use clap_complete::{generate, Generator, Shell};

fn generate_completions<G: Generator>(gen: G, cmd: &mut Command, name: &str, out: &mut dyn Write) {
    generate(gen, cmd, name, out);
}

// CLI command to generate completions
#[derive(Subcommand)]
enum CompletionCommands {
    /// Generate shell completions
    Completions {
        #[arg(value_enum)]
        shell: Shell,
    },
}

fn handle_completions(shell: Shell) {
    let mut cmd = Cli::command();
    match shell {
        Shell::Bash => generate_completions(Shell::Bash, &mut cmd, "br", &mut io::stdout()),
        Shell::Zsh => generate_completions(Shell::Zsh, &mut cmd, "br", &mut io::stdout()),
        Shell::Fish => generate_completions(Shell::Fish, &mut cmd, "br", &mut io::stdout()),
        Shell::PowerShell => generate_completions(Shell::PowerShell, &mut cmd, "br", &mut io::stdout()),
        _ => eprintln!("Unsupported shell"),
    }
}
```

### Dynamic Completions
For dynamic values like issue IDs:
```rust
// Custom completer for issue IDs (zsh/fish support)
fn complete_issue_ids() -> Vec<String> {
    // Read from cache file or query DB
    if let Ok(contents) = fs::read_to_string(".beads/.id_cache") {
        contents.lines().map(|s| s.to_string()).collect()
    } else {
        Vec::new()
    }
}
```

### Installation Instructions
```
# Bash (add to ~/.bashrc)
eval "$(br completions bash)"

# Zsh (add to ~/.zshrc)
eval "$(br completions zsh)"

# Fish (add to ~/.config/fish/config.fish)
br completions fish | source

# PowerShell (add to $PROFILE)
br completions powershell | Out-String | Invoke-Expression
```

## Completions Provided

### Command Completions
```
br <TAB>
create  update  close  reopen  list  show  ready  blocked
dep     label   comment search  stats sync  config  doctor  prime
```

### Option Completions
```
br create --<TAB>
--title  --type  --priority  --assignee  --labels  --deps
--description  --parent  --due  --json  --robot

br list --<TAB>
--status  --type  --priority  --assignee  --labels
--sort  --limit  --json
```

### Value Completions
```
br create --type=<TAB>
bug  feature  task  epic  question  docs  chore

br list --status=<TAB>
open  in_progress  closed  all

br show <TAB>
beads_rust-abc123  beads_rust-def456  beads_rust-ghi789
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Generate bash completions
- [ ] Generate zsh completions
- [ ] Generate fish completions
- [ ] Generate PowerShell completions
- [ ] Complete command names
- [ ] Complete option names
- [ ] Complete option values (enum types)
- [ ] Dynamic issue ID completion (if feasible)
- [ ] Installation instructions in --help

## Dependencies
- Requires `clap_complete` crate (already in Cargo.toml)
- Requires CLI Skeleton (Command definition)

## Rationale
Shell completions dramatically improve CLI usability. Users can discover available commands and options via tab completion. Dynamic issue ID completion saves typing and reduces errors.
